name: Alignment Guides Intent
summary: Peer-relative magnetic alignment — Figma-style smart guides: as an element is moved or resized, it snaps to the edges/centers of its peer elements (and the container), rendering transient guide lines at the match (Fork 3b of #1384). A STANDALONE concern because its reference frame is peer-relative and needs no grid — distinct from grid-snap (a dimension of arrangeable, which quantizes to fixed cells). Composes with resizable, arrangeable, and free-canvas drag: it observes the moving element against its peers and biases the position toward alignment within a snap threshold.

reference.description: What the moving element aligns against — `peers` snaps to sibling elements' edges/centers (Figma smart guides); `container` snaps to the bounding container's edges/center/margins; `both` (default) considers peers and the container together. The reference frame is relative, never a fixed grid (that is arrangeable's `snap`).

edges.description: Which features of the elements participate in a match — `bounds` aligns left/right/top/bottom edges; `centers` aligns horizontal/vertical centerlines; `both` (default) matches edges and centers, the Figma behavior. Equal-spacing/distribution guides extend this set.

guides.description: Whether the transient alignment lines are rendered while dragging — `shown` (default) draws the guide line + (optionally) the equal-spacing pips at the match; `hidden` snaps silently with no rendered line (the magnetism still applies). The snap THRESHOLD (how near a match must be to engage, in px) is a numeric property of the binding, not an enum value.

description:
What This Is

Magnetic peer alignment — Figma-style smart guides (Fork 3b of #1384). As an element is moved or resized, it snaps to the edges and centers of its peer elements (and the container), and a transient guide line is drawn at each match. The single most load-bearing finding of the spatial survey: this is a standalone capability, not a facet of the 2-D grid model.

Why standalone — peer-relative, needs no grid

"Snap" is two structurally different things. Grid-snap quantizes a position to fixed cells — it is a dimension of arrangeable. Magnetic alignment has a peer-relative reference frame (this element vs those elements) and works on a free canvas with no grid at all. The two never reduce to each other, so alignment-guides is its own intent that composes the spatial models rather than living inside one.

Dimensions

  • reference: peers / container / both (default) — what to align against.
  • edges: bounds / centers / both (default) — which features match.
  • guides: shown (default, draw the line) vs hidden (snap silently). The snap threshold (engage distance, px) is a numeric binding property, not an enum.

Composition

Composes with resizable (align a dragged divider/edge), arrangeable (alignment on a free-placement canvas, orthogonal to its grid-snap), and free-canvas drag generally. The drag itself is recognized via the gesture intent (pan); alignment-guides bias the resulting position. Lead incumbents: moveable, interact.js. Grounded in the spatial-manipulation survey (spatial-manipulation arrangeable surfaces).
